subject: Ecology
title: Eradication Success Stories
description: A quick, practical guide to Eradication Success Stories.

hook.text: On Macquarie Island, removing rats and rabbits brought back seabirds that hadn't bred there in decades.

buildup.text: South Georgia Island eradicated rats across 3,500 square kilometers—the largest removal ever.

discovery.text: Native pipits and pintails returned within months of the rats disappearing.

twist.text: Eradication requires getting every last individual. One pregnant rat can restart the invasion.

climax.text: Over 1,200 successful island eradications worldwide prove the approach works at scale.

punchline.text: Zero tolerance is the only eradication that works.

quiz.question: Why must island eradication remove every invasive individual?
options: One survivor can restart the invasion
Partial removal has the same benefit
